name: Kubernetes Cluster Setup
description: Provision and configure a production-ready Kubernetes cluster with networking, storage, security, and observability foundations.
triggers: setup k8s with networking and storage

inputs.name: cloud_provider
inputs.description: Target cloud provider (AWS, GCP, Azure) and region.

inputs.name: cluster_spec
inputs.description: Node count, instance types, Kubernetes version, and networking CIDR.

inputs.name: security_requirements
inputs.description: RBAC policies, pod security standards, and network policy requirements.

inputs.name: storage_requirements
inputs.description: Storage classes, persistent volume needs, and backup strategy.

outputs.name: cluster_manifest
outputs.description: Infrastructure-as-code definitions for the provisioned cluster.

outputs.name: networking_config
outputs.description: CNI plugin configuration, ingress controller setup, and network policies.

outputs.name: access_credentials
outputs.description: kubeconfig, RBAC roles, and service account definitions.

outputs.name: validation_report
outputs.description: Cluster health checks, node readiness, and conformance test results.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is cluster provisioning, not application deployment or Helm chart authoring.
Require cloud provider credentials and region selection before provisioning resources.
Validate each provisioned component passes health checks before proceeding to the next step.
Return cluster manifest, networking config, access credentials, and validation report with rollback instructions.

steps.description: Define node pools, instance types, Kubernetes version, and CIDR ranges based on cluster_spec and cloud_provider.
Create the managed or self-managed control plane with API server, etcd, and scheduler configuration.
Install CNI plugin, configure ingress controller, set up DNS, and apply baseline network policies.
Provision storage classes, configure CSI drivers, and validate persistent volume provisioning.
Apply RBAC policies, pod security standards, secrets management, and audit logging configuration.
Run conformance tests, node readiness checks, and end-to-end smoke tests against the cluster.
Generate kubeconfig, runbook, and operational documentation for cluster consumers.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read existing IaC templates, cluster configs, and security policies.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write cluster manifests, networking configs, and documentation.

tools.name: shell.exec
tools.purpose: Execute kubectl, cloud CLI, and conformance test commands.

verification.method: All nodes report Ready status via kubectl get nodes.
Baseline network policies are in place and tested.
Persistent volume claims bind successfully within 60 seconds.
Kubernetes conformance test suite passes with zero failures.

success_criteria: Cluster is provisioned with all nodes in Ready state.
Networking, storage, and security are configured per specification.
Conformance tests pass and validation report is generated.
Rollback procedure is documented and tested.

rollback.when: If cluster validation fails critical checks, tear down provisioned resources and revert IaC state.
